rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new declarations.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: Boolean expressions for bool types, numeric for int/real types.
Move invalid implementation-level guarantees to the correct implementation scope or remove them.
Do not add new package or data type references that are not present in the model.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers in their scope.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions should be resolved by aligning helper declarations with actual expressions.
Implementation-level bindings should only reference valid outputs or declared auxiliary variables.
Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be addressed by removing or rewriting clauses.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must preserve type consistency; e.g., bool expressions for bool variables.
Use only declared constants or equations in the same annex for expressions.
Assignments in implementation scope must target valid output features or auxiliary variables.
Component type annexes should not contain implementation-level references or bindings.
anti_patterns: Adding new package or data type references not present in the model.
Using unresolved identifiers in AGREE expressions without fixing the root cause.
Placing implementation-level guarantees in component type annexes.
Introducing new declarations to resolve validator errors instead of using visible identifiers.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE annex placement in component types vs. implementations
